narrative:
First Warn Forecast
Sunday, June 7, 2026

Headlines

Scattered showers and thunderstorms are expected today and Monday, with locally heavy rainfall possible.
A few storms this afternoon and evening across central and southeastern Missouri may produce isolated damaging wind gusts.
Heat and humidity build into the region Tuesday through Thursday, with temperatures climbing into the 90s and heat index values nearing 100 degrees.

A slow-moving upper-level disturbance will continue to influence the weather across the Mid-Mississippi Valley today and Monday. As temperatures warm through the afternoon, scattered showers and thunderstorms will develop across Missouri and Illinois. While not everyone will see rain, those that do can expect brief heavy downpours, lightning, and gusty winds.

The greatest concern today will be across central and southeastern Missouri, where the atmosphere will become unstable enough to support a few stronger thunderstorms. Isolated damaging wind gusts will be the primary severe weather threat. Large hail is not expected to be a widespread concern, and the risk for tornadoes remains very low. Any thunderstorm, however, will be capable of producing locally heavy rainfall that could lead to ponding of water on roads and reduced visibility.

Shower and thunderstorm activity will gradually diminish this evening, although a few isolated showers may linger overnight. Another round of scattered showers and thunderstorms is expected Monday as the weather system slowly weakens and moves east. While locally heavy rain remains possible, the overall severe weather threat appears lower than what is expected today.

By Tuesday, a significant pattern change begins as high pressure strengthens across the Midwest. This will usher in the first prolonged stretch of summer-like heat and humidity of the season. Temperatures will climb into the lower and middle 90s through midweek, while increasing humidity will push heat index values close to 100 degrees. The hottest conditions are expected Wednesday and Thursday, making outdoor activities more strenuous and increasing the risk of heat-related illness for those spending extended periods outside.

Relief from the heat appears likely toward the end of the week as a cold front approaches the region. That front will bring renewed chances for showers and thunderstorms late Thursday into Friday, followed by somewhat cooler temperatures heading into next weekend.

First. Accurate. Trusted.
